concept.title: Plugin Lifecycle
concept.shortdesc: Follow a lifecycle that starts with observed migration gaps and ends with validated plugin rollout.

image.alt: Lifecycle from gap identification to pattern design, action emission, handler execution, and report review.
Decision points for when a migration issue requires a plugin or core-compatible routing.

section.title: Who, what, where, when, and why

strow.stentry: Engineers who own migration edge cases for a corpus and can validate behavior with fixtures.
An installable package that implements DitaPlugin and contributes one or more extension points.
In the same Python environment as dita_package_processor, discovered by entry points.
When recurring migration cases are not solved by core action types and routing logic.
To keep custom migration behavior deterministic, testable, and auditable.

ol.li: Why is the migration failing or producing low-quality structure? Identify the observed artifact pattern.
Why does core planning not route this case correctly? Identify missing evidence or missing routing logic.
Why can this not be solved with existing action types alone? Determine if custom handler semantics are required.
Why is this case recurring? Validate across multiple fixture packages.
Why is a plugin the correct boundary? Confirm ownership, test coverage plan, and rollout process.
